validation.stdout_sample:
usage: compute_gc_content.py [-h] --sequence SEQUENCE [--output {json,text}]

Compute GC content (fraction) of a DNA sequence.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --sequence SEQUENCE   DNA sequence string to analyze (e.g., ACGTACGT).
  --output {json,text}  Output format.

usage: find_connected_components.py [-h]
                                    (--edge EDGE | --edges-json EDGES_JSON | --edges-json-file EDGES_JSON_FILE)
                                    [--delimiter DELIMITER] [--sort]
                                    [--output {json,text}]

Find connected components in an undirected graph from an edge list.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --edge EDGE           An edge specified as 'LEFT,RIGHT' (repeatable).
  --edges-json EDGES_JSON
                        JSON string representing a list of 2-item edges, e.g.
                        '[["a","b"],["c","d"]]'.
  --edges-json-file EDGES_JSON_FILE
                        Path to a JSON file containing a list of 2-item edges.
  --delimiter DELIMITER
                        Delimiter used in --edge values (default: ',').
  --sort                Sort nodes within each component for stable output.
  --output {json,text}  Output format (default: json).

usage: reverse_complement_dna [-h] --sequence SEQUENCE [--output {json,text}]

Compute the reverse complement of a DNA sequence.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --sequence SEQUENCE   Input DNA sequence (A,C,G,T; case preserved for these
                        bases).
  --output {json,text}  Output format.

usage: summarize_numeric_columns.py [-h] [--input-json INPUT_JSON]
                                    [--include INCLUDE] [--exclude EXCLUDE]
                                    [--allow-numeric-strings]
                                    [--output {json,text}] [--pretty]

Summarize numeric columns across a JSON array of row objects. Reads from
--input-json or stdin.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --input-json INPUT_JSON
                        JSON string representing a list of row objects
                        (list[dict]). If omitted or empty, read JSON from
                        stdin.
  --include INCLUDE     Comma-separated list of column names to include
                        (optional).
  --exclude EXCLUDE     Comma-separated list of column names to exclude
                        (optional).
  --allow-numeric-strings
                        Treat numeric-looking strings (e.g., '3.14') as
                        numbers.
  --output {json,text}  Output format.
  --pretty              Pretty-print JSON output (only applies to --output
                        json).
